codeReview.summary: This is a verification-only feature with no code changes. The worker successfully verified that both API endpoints (/api/calls and /api/calls/stats) return live data from the Google Sheet with correct structure, proper reverse-chronological sorting, and all expected fields. The existing implementation was exercised and validated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from the fullstack-worker skill procedure (step 1.2 Initialize Environment) by using a PowerShell equivalent instead of .factory/init.sh, which is appropriate since bash is not available on Windows hosts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback shows followedProcedure: false with deviation for step 1.2. Worker ran PowerShell equivalent: 'if (!(Test-Path node_modules)... npm install'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill doesn't have a clear branch for verification-only features. Worker correctly identified this was verification work and skipped TDD steps, but the skill feedback suggests this deviation should be formally supported.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations shows worker skipped 'Write Tests First (TDD)' step because 'feature scope is verification-only - no new code to write'. Suggested change: 'Add a branch in the skill for verification-only worker runs when assigned feature implementation already exists'.

summary: Verification feature completed successfully. Worker started the dev server, hit both /api/calls and /api/calls/stats endpoints, and confirmed they return HTTP 200 with properly structured live data from Google Sheets. /api/calls returns 3 call objects with all expected fields (date, name, number, request, callStatus, callDuration, etc.) sorted reverse-chronologically. /api/calls/stats returns complete stats object with totalCalls, answeredCount, voicemailCount, qualifiedLeads, averageDuration, and 14-entry callsPerDay array. All existing tests pass (35 tests), TypeScript check passes, lint passes. Non-blocking observation: some columns in live sheet have null values (callStatus, financingInterest, creditScore, callDuration), which is a data quality issue in the source sheet, not a code bug. No code changes required - this was purely runtime verification as designed.
